summary: Analysis of triage data reveals that the WhatsApp desktop application is experiencing network interception, likely due to a captive portal or transparent proxy. While initial findings focused on DNS resolution failures (e.g., 'The remote name could not be resolved: 'web.whatsapp.com''), a deeper review of 'main-process.log.0' uncovered a critical 'Invalid release entry: <!DOCTYPE html>' error. This indicates the application is receiving HTML content instead of the expected update manifest. The DNS errors are likely a symptom of this broader network interception. Additionally, the 'Network Action Predictor' database requires further investigation to correlate URL prediction history with these failed update attempts.

artifacts.name: WhatsApp Main Process Log (Error)
artifacts.description: Log file containing critical 'Invalid release entry: <!DOCTYPE html>' error, suggesting network interception.

artifacts.name: WhatsApp Main Process Log (DNS)
artifacts.description: Log file containing repeated 'The remote name could not be resolved: 'web.whatsapp.com'' errors, likely a symptom of network interception.

artifacts.name: Network Action Predictor
artifacts.description: SQLite database containing URL prediction and network history, to be correlated with failed update attempts.

artifacts.name: WhatsApp Auto Updater Error
artifacts.value: pid: 9940, msg: 'Auto updater: error [Error: Command failed: 4294967295]'
artifacts.description: Process ID 9940 repeatedly failing update checks.

artifacts.name: NetworkUXBroker Prefetch
artifacts.description: Prefetch file for NetworkUXBroker.exe, potentially related to network activity.

artifacts.name: Flash Cookie
artifacts.description: Flash cookie found in Macromedia directory, indicating potential tracking or ad-capping activity.
